note: Replay plan events were derived from effective adapter replay_events generated from source test evidence.

events.description: Create object, add empty array, retrieve and verify type
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c lines 466: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(array = cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(root, "array")); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(array->type, cJSON_Array)

input.case: Create JsonValue object, insert an empty array under key "array"

expected.observable_behavior: Object contains an array entry with type array

events.description: Create object, add true and false bools, retrieve and verify types
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c lines 446: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(true_item = cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(root, "true")); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(true_item->type, cJSON_True); similar for false

input.case: Create JsonValue object, insert Boolean(true) under "true" and Boolean(false) under "false"

expected.observable_behavior: Object contains boolean entries with correct true/false types

events.description: Create object, add false bool, retrieve and verify type
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c lines 442: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(false_item = cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive(root, "false")); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(false_item->type, cJSON_False)

input.case: Create JsonValue object, insert Boolean(false) under key "false"

expected.observable_behavior: Object contains a boolean entry with false type

events.description: Create object, add null, retrieve and verify type

events.description: Add a number to an object and verify type and value
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c:450: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(type); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_DOUBLE(valuedouble); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(valueint)

input.case: Create object, add number 42 with key 'number', retrieve and check

expected.observable_behavior: After adding number 42 to object with key 'number', the item exists, has type Number, valuedouble=42, valueint=42.

events.description: Add an empty object to an object and verify type
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c:462: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(type == cJSON_Object)

input.case: Create object, add empty object with key 'object', retrieve and check type

expected.observable_behavior: After adding empty object to object with key 'object', the item exists and has type Object.

events.description: Add a raw string to an object and verify value
events.evidence: tests/cjson_add.c:458: TEST_ASSERT_NOT_NULL(cJSON_GetObjectItemCaseSensitive); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_INT(type == cJSON_Raw); TEST_ASSERT_EQUAL_STRING(valuestring == "{}")

input.case: Create object, add raw string "{}" with key 'raw', retrieve and check type and value

expected.observable_behavior: After adding raw string "{}" to object with key 'raw', the item exists, has type Raw, and valuestring equals "{}".

events.description: Replay JSON array comparison behavior from source test cjson_compare_should_compare_arrays.
events.evidence: tests/compare_tests.c lines 204-215, helper compare_from_string. Source test asserts true/false from compare_from_string calls with various array JSON strings.

event_metadata.normalization: Observable comparison rule: the equality result from JsonValue::eq must match the boolean returned by cJSON_Compare.

input.case: compare various JSON arrays (empty, nested, different order, prefix)

expected.observable_behavior: The Rust test should return true when comparing equal arrays and false when arrays differ, matching the source test assertions.

events.description: Replay JSON boolean comparison behavior from source test cjson_compare_should_compare_booleans.
events.evidence: tests/compare_tests.c lines 199-211, helper compare_from_string. Source test asserts true/false from compare_from_string calls with boolean JSON strings.

event_metadata.normalization: Observable comparison rule: the equality result from JsonValue::eq must match the boolean returned by cJSON_Compare.

input.case: compare boolean values true/false

expected.observable_behavior: The Rust test should return true when comparing identical booleans and false otherwise, matching the source test assertions.

input.case: Create object, add string 'Hello World!' under key 'string', then retrieve.

expected.observable_behavior: The retrieved value contains the string 'Hello World!'.

expected.observable_behavior: Two nulls are equal; null and true are not equal.

events.description: Compare numeric JSON values with various equalities and inequalities

input.case: Parse and compare number strings: same numbers, different numbers, scientific notation

events.description: Compare JSON objects with key order, case sensitivity, and subset checks
events.evidence: tests/compare_tests.c line 205: multiple compare_from_string calls with objects

input.case: Parse and compare object strings with various key differences

expected.observable_behavior: Equal objects (same keys/values) are equal; objects with different key order are equal (cJSON behavior); case-sensitive compares differ on key case; case-insensitive compares treat 'False' and 'false' as equal; objects with different keys are not equal.

events.description: Compare raw JSON values (strings containing JSON) by parsing and using eq
events.evidence: tests/compare_tests.c line 203: parse raw string, set type to cJSON_Raw, compare expecting true

input.case: Parse string '"[true, false]"' twice and compare

expected.observable_behavior: Two identical raw items (strings) compare equal.

events.description: Compare JSON string values
events.evidence: tests/compare_tests.c line 202: compare_from_string with strings

input.case: Parse '"abcdefg"' and compare to itself and to '"ABCDEFG"'

expected.observable_behavior: Equal strings compare equal; different case strings compare not equal (even with case_sensitive=false, source expects false because the library's case-insensitive mode is not implemented in target? Actually source test expects both true and false? Wait source test: TEST_ASSERT_FALSE for compare_from_string("\"ABCDEFG\"", "\"abcdefg\"", true); and also TEST_ASSERT_FALSE for case_sensitive=false? Source test has both true and false? Looking at source: it has TEST_ASSERT_TRUE for both case_sensitive true/false for same case strings, then TEST_ASSERT_FALSE for different case strings with case_sensitive true and also false? Actually the source test: TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(compare_from_string("\"abcdefg\"", "\"abcdefg\"", true)); TEST_ASSERT_TRUE(compare_from_string("\"abcdefg\"", "\"abcdefg\"", false)); TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(compare_from_string("\"ABCDEFG\"", "\"abcdefg\"", true)); TEST_ASSERT_FALSE(compare_from_string("\"ABCDEFG\"", "\"abcdefg\"", false)); So even with case_sensitive false, it still returns FALSE because the strings are different? That seems odd; maybe the library's case-insensitive mode only affects keys, not string values? Actually cJSON_Compare's case_sensitive parameter: if false, string comparisons are case-insensitive? The documentation? Not sure. The source test expects false even for case-insensitive comparing "ABCDEFG" vs "abcdefg". That suggests case-insensitive mode does not apply to string values? Or maybe it does but the test expects false? Wait, the test asserts FALSE, so the library returns false for that comparison even with case_sensitive=false. So we follow source: both comparisons are false. In Rust, eq is case-sensitive, so both comparisons are false. So we can write assert_ne! for both cases. That matches source. Good.
